critic_raw:
CRITIQUE (Round 1):

1. [ISSUE-A] SUPERVISION_SOURCE_MISMATCH: Neural retriever trained on impression data with skip/non-consumption labels; baseline trained on positive-play-only table. Different supervision sources, not equivalent training regimes.
2. [ISSUE-B] FEATURE_SET_MISMATCH: Audio-content embeddings and skip behavior in retriever; only play events in baseline. Input feature asymmetry.
3. [ISSUE-C] NO_CONTENT_AWARE_BASELINE: No impression-aware matrix factorization or content-aware shallow model tested.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (Round 1):
[ISSUE-A] REBUT: Impression data and skip labels are the training data the team has for the new system. The baseline uses the available legacy data.
[ISSUE-B] REBUT: Audio embeddings are a legitimate feature. Better features are part of a better model.
[ISSUE-C] REBUT: Matrix factorization is the incumbent. Comparing against incumbent is standard practice.
adjudication_raw:
Round 1:
- [ISSUE-A]: OPEN — defender's argument ('impression data is what we have') doesn't address the scientific claim. If the paper claims neural retrieval outperforms matrix factorization, that requires the same training data for both.
- [ISSUE-B]: OPEN — 'better features are part of a better model' is a deployment claim, not a neural-architecture quality claim. The paper makes an architecture claim.
- [ISSUE-C]: OPEN — 'incumbent is standard' does not address the ablation need.

Round 2:
Critic [ISSUE-A]: For the claim 'neural retrieval outperforms matrix factorization' to be valid, both need equivalent training data. Training one on impression data with negatives and one on positive-only data tests training regime, not architecture.
Defender [ISSUE-A]: Concede — the claimed contribution (neural retrieval) cannot be isolated when supervision differs. The paper overreaches in its framing.
[RESOLVED: ISSUE-A — conceded]

Critic [ISSUE-B]: Audio embeddings and skip behavior are inputs unavailable to the baseline. These are not 'part of the architecture'; they are separate inputs.
Defender [ISSUE-B]: Concede — feature set differences compound with supervision differences to inflate the apparent advantage.
[RESOLVED: ISSUE-B — conceded]

Critic [ISSUE-C]: An impression-aware matrix factorization with the same inputs would answer the architecture question directly.
Defender [ISSUE-C]: Concede — the absence of this baseline leaves the architecture contribution unquantified.
[RESOLVED: ISSUE-C — conceded]

All three issues conceded by defender. Verdict: critique_wins.
